title: cleistopholis albida
intro: Cleistopholis albida, also known as the white bush mango, is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a plant native to tropical Africa, particularly in the regions of West and Central Africa, where it is often found growing in the wild. The medicinal properties of Cleistopholis albida have been utilized for various health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for herbal remedies, and its leaves and fruits are used in traditional medicine.
